description: The rabbit: the soft quick animal of meadow and garden edge, wild or kept. Built on 'tupi' (jump) — the jumper, opened wide by 'wa' — so the animal is named by its signature motion, as 'mophira' is named by its wool. Counted with 'lipha'.
sound_symbolism: 'tupi' springs and 'wa' lands it open — the word performs one hop. All light sounds; nothing in it heavier than the animal.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'tupiwa mua whelina tupi.' (The rabbit jumps in the grass.) 'wi waeshi tupiwa' (two wild rabbits). The Velveteen Rabbit text is its first home, and 'waeshi tupiwa' (wild rabbit) its standing contrast.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The hedgerow neighbor every garden knew: quick to raid, quick to vanish, the wild's small ambassador at the edge of the tended world.
